business.name: Master Sanctum Restoration
business.description: Luxury natural stone and marble restoration — architectural marble, heritage stone, countertops, monuments, and sanctuaries of craft.

guarantees: Written conservation assessment before work begins
Impregnating sealers and documented care protocols

pillars.title: Assessment
pillars.subtitle: Every surface tells a story
pillars.desc: We document substrate, veining, prior repairs, and environmental exposure before a single tool touches stone. Conservation discipline starts with listening to what the material remembers.

pillars.title: Restoration
pillars.subtitle: Hand-finished under protocol
pillars.desc: Controlled honing, crack stabilization, color-matched infill, and lippage removal — each pass executed by hand, not contractor haste. Depth, clarity, and architectural integrity restored.

pillars.title: Protection
pillars.subtitle: Sealed for permanence
pillars.desc: Impregnating sealers, maintenance protocols, and documented care guidance so your stone endures generations — not just the next season.

whyChoose.headline: Why Choose Master Sanctum
whyChoose.philosophy: Protecting What Endures is not a tagline — it is the standard every commission is held to.

highlights.title: 20+ Years of Stone Craft
highlights.desc: Two decades restoring marble, limestone, granite, and heritage surfaces for residential estates, commercial landmarks, and memorial commissions.

highlights.title: Disney & Luxury Hospitality
highlights.desc: Commissioned work across high-traffic hospitality environments where finish durability and guest-facing presentation demand conservation-grade discipline.

highlights.title: NYC Architectural Stone
highlights.desc: Experience with urban luxury interiors — honed vanities, lobby floors, and architectural marble where lippage, etching, and wear require precision, not shortcuts.

highlights.title: Residential · Commercial · Historical
highlights.desc: From kitchen countertops and shower surrounds to monument conservation and exterior cladding — one protocol, every substrate.

marmorax.name: Marmorax
marmorax.heroTeaser: Guarded by Marmorax — a marble knight who awakens when stone is assessed, restored, and sealed with conservation discipline.
marmorax.wardenTeaser: Carved from living veined marble to stand watch over sacred architecture, Marmorax fell silent for generations — until the first honest honing pass called him back to vigil.
marmorax.imageAlt: Marmorax, the Eternal Warden of Sanctum — marble gargoyle knight emblem

timeline.title: Origin Legend
timeline.desc: Master stonecutters shaped Marmorax from a single block of Carrara — not as ornament, but as oath. His wings were folded into the parapet; his eyes set to catch the last light on polished limestone.

timeline.title: Ancient Cathedral
timeline.desc: For hundreds of years he watched from the clerestory — rain, incense, and foot-worn marble passing beneath his gaze. Every surface he protected was assessed before touched, finished by hand, sealed against time.

timeline.title: Collapse & Dormancy
timeline.desc: War, neglect, and acid rain took the spire. Marmorax shattered into the rubble field and slept within the dust of his own material — memory locked in veined fragments.

timeline.title: Reawakening at Master Sanctum
timeline.desc: When Master Sanctum Restoration was founded in Florida, the first conservation commission — a honed Carrara vanity — sent a pulse through the stone. Marmorax opened his eyes. Gold light returned to the sanctum.

legend.title: Origin Legend
paragraphs: Before Master Sanctum bore a name, there was only the material — marble with memory. Craftsmen of the old cathedral schools believed certain blocks held vigilance: stone that had witnessed enough prayer and weather to want guarding in return.
Marmorax was carved in that spirit — a gargoyle knight whose armor is veining, whose shield is the MSR crest, whose purpose is singular: protect what endures.

legend.title: Ancient Cathedral
paragraphs: He was set upon a high parapet where Atlantic winds met limestone and marble alike. From that height he learned the discipline still used today: document before intervention, honor the substrate, never replace what can be conserved.
Pilgrims below saw only weathered stone. The warden saw stories — etch rings on thresholds, lippage on nave floors, hairline fractures waiting to become loss.

legend.title: The Fall
paragraphs: Centuries erode even the faithful. Scaffolding came down and never returned. The cathedral roof failed; Marmorax fell with the cornice he once guarded.
In the rubble he dreamed in mineral time — slow, layered, patient — until the craft he embodied nearly disappeared from the world.

legend.title: Reawakening
paragraphs: Florida humidity, salt air, and sun load became his new cathedral. Estates, monuments, hospitality lobbies, and kitchen sanctuaries — each commission a parapet restored.
Marmorax stirs when assessment is honest and finish is by hand. His eyes glow gold at the final seal. The MSR shield is his crest upon every protected surface.

legend.title: The Warden's Oath
paragraphs: I will assess before I strike. I will restore without haste. I will seal for permanence, not for the season.
This is the oath behind Protecting What Endures — and the standard Master Sanctum Restoration keeps on every commission guarded by Marmorax.

brandVoice: Every sealed commission carries the warden's crest — a quiet mark of conservation discipline.
Marmorax embodies the MSR method: Assessment, Restoration, Protection.
From Florida estates to heritage monuments nationwide — the knight watches what endures.
marmorax.floridaHeritage: Reawakened in Florida — humidity, salt air, and sun load are his new cathedral. Every MSR commission is a parapet restored under the warden's vigil.

services.title: Marble Restoration
services.desc: Vanities, floors, and architectural marble — honed, polished, and sealed.

services.title: Memorial & Monument
services.desc: Heritage stone cleaning, stabilization, and long-horizon protection.

services.title: Kitchen & Bath Stone
services.desc: Etch removal, crack infill, and color-matched refinishing.

services.title: Exterior Stone
services.desc: Weather-damaged cladding, benches, and facades restored to endure.

reviews.author: Sarah M.
reviews.text: Our Carrara vanity looked destroyed after years of etching. MSR returned it to a depth and clarity I didn't think was possible.

reviews.author: James T.
reviews.text: Conservation-minded from the first visit. They assessed before touching anything — exactly the discipline we wanted for our memorial stone.

reviews.author: Elena R.
reviews.text: Kitchen marble restored without the rushed contractor feel. Hand-finished, documented, and sealed. Still looks flawless months later.

gatekeeper.headline: Begin Under Protocol
gatekeeper.subheadline: Every commission starts with assessment — not haste.
gatekeeper.teaser: The Gatekeeper reviews your inquiry, asks the questions that matter, and routes qualified projects to an on-site conservation assessment.

steps.title: Intake
steps.desc: Download the client questionnaire or submit your project details — stone type, location, primary concerns, and photos if available.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.desc: We clarify substrate, exposure, timeline, and realistic outcomes. No pressure — useful guidance even if you're still researching.

steps.title: Site Assessment
steps.desc: Qualified commissions receive a 30–45 minute on-site visit. You receive a written scope and estimate — hand-finished protocol, no shortcut contractors.

discoveryQuestions: What surfaces need restoration? (countertop, vanity, floor, shower, monument, exterior)
Stone type — marble, granite, limestone, travertine, or unknown?
Primary concerns — etching, stains, scratches, dullness, cracks, lippage?
Can you provide photos? (wide shot + worst-area close-up)
gatekeeper.serviceAreaNote: On-site assessments across SWFL — humidity, salt air, and sun load are our daily cathedral.

faqs.question: What is the client intake questionnaire?
faqs.answer: A downloadable form that captures stone type, location, damage, dimensions, timeline, and photos — so we can assess your project before any tool touches stone. Access it at /intake or from the quote section.

faqs.question: How do I schedule a site assessment?
faqs.answer: Submit the intake questionnaire or request a quote with project details. The Gatekeeper confirms qualified leads and schedules a 30–45 minute on-site assessment. You'll receive a calendar confirmation and written scope after the visit.

faqs.question: What types of stone do you restore?
faqs.answer: Marble, limestone, granite, travertine, and architectural heritage stone — vanities, floors, monuments, cladding, and countertops.

faqs.question: Do you serve areas outside Florida?
faqs.answer: Yes. We are based in Florida and accept select commissions nationwide for conservation-grade restoration projects.

faqs.question: How is a conservation quote prepared?
faqs.answer: Every project begins with an on-site or detailed assessment. We document condition, scope, and protocol before providing a written estimate.

faqs.question: What is your satisfaction guarantee?
faqs.answer: We stand behind our hand-finished work with documented protocols, impregnating sealers, and a satisfaction commitment on every commission.

faqs.question: How long does a typical restoration take?
faqs.answer: Timelines vary by stone type and scope. Vanity and countertop work is often completed in days; larger architectural commissions are scheduled by phase.
